title: veronica atlantica
intro: Veronica atlantica, also known as the Atlantic speedwell or Atlantic gypsywort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Veronica genus. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, Veronica atlantica has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region.
